text:
In a quiet town where the seasons changed by the inch rather than the calendar, lived an eccentric inventor named Leonard Fumblebottom. Leonard spent his days in his peculiar workshop, filled with contraptions that resembled inventions from childhood fantasies blended seamlessly with the more outlandish of folk tales. His latest invention was the "Eternal Yawner," which boasted the most peculiar ability: it could make any person instantly yawn until they resembled an old lullaby with sunken eyes.

On a particularly dreary Thursday, with rain pelting the windows of the humble, cobblestoned shop in the heart of the village, Leonard invited a collection of townsfolk to his workshop for what he assured them was an exhibition of his latest marvel. The main draw was the Eternal Yawner. Little did they know, Leonard had brought extra doses for himself along with a smattering of volunteers.

The room was dense with the sounds of rain and anticipation, laughter, and the occasional yawn attempting to break
